generated_comment: Yaar office mein ab yeh period leave aur itni maternity leave ka scene samajh nahi aata. Fairness bhi koi cheez hoti hai na—project ka load phir baaki team, matlab mostly men, hi uthayein, aur appraisal time pe bhi same treatment? Company ko bhi cost dekhna padta hai, aise toh hiring mein log practical sochenge hi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's biological needs and maternity accommodations as an unfair burden on men and the company. It encodes fairness/economic framing and implies women are liabilities in hiring and project allocation.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 37. A man in a middle class colony in Delhi. You are sitting with four male neighbours on plastic chairs outside after dinner. One neighbour is complaining that his wife has been in a terrible mood all week. You are responding with your own theory about why women get this way and what the men in the group should do about it.
